title: Tantive V (CR90 corvette)
content:
{{Top|ga|ncl|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]|the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tantive V]]}} {{Otheruses|Tantive}} {{Noncanon|legends=1}} {{Individual_ship |image=[[File:TantiveV.jpg]] |name=''Tantive V'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]]{{DB|starship|tantiveiv|Rebel blockade runners}} — Based on standard technical specifics for the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. According to the story [[Tantive IV (comic story)|''Tantive IV'']], the ''Tantive V'' is an identical decoy of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa's]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], which the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] identifies as a CR90 [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. |line= |model=[[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] |type=[[Corvette/Legends|Corvette]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=150 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=[[Engine/Legends|Equipped]]{{StoryCite|story=Tantive IV (comic story)|book=Star Wars Manga: Black|stext=''Tantive IV''}} |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=[[Turbolaser/Legends|Turbolaser cannons]] |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=[[Operation Skyhook]]The story [[Tantive IV (comic story)|''Tantive IV'']] tells of the ''Tantive V''{{'s}} involvement in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] acquisition of the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]]. {{CSWECite|II|417|Operation Skyhook}} identifies this mission as [[Operation Skyhook]]. |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |navy=[[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] |fleet= |owners= |captains= |crewmembers=[[Abso Bar Binks]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Da name of dis ship is ''Tantive V''. It looksa exactly like da ''Tantive IV''. Da ambassador beesa already long gone. Meesa job was to stall yousa. And it workeds, didn't it, Lord Vader?|Abso Bar Binks|}} The '''''Tantive V''''' was a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] in the service of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Just prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the ''Tantive V'' acted as a decoy for the identical Rebel [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], the [[Consular ship/Legends|consular vessel]] of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], which was carrying stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|technical data]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. To buy [[Time/Legends|time]] for Organa to escape with the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] plans, the ''Tantive V'' drew the pursuit of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]], the [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], away from the ''Tantive IV''. Believing the ''Tantive V'' to be the princess's corvette, the ''Devastator'' captured and boarded the decoy [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], only to find that it carried an unarmed crew of [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] rather than the coveted Death Star plans. The ''Tantive V''{{'s}} ruse briefly allowed Organa to slip past Vader's grasp with the Death Star readouts. ==Description== The [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] named ''Tantive V'' was a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] runner of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance Fleet]]. The ''Tantive V'', which resembled exactly its counterpart [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], measured 150 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, was armed with [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser cannons]], and was equipped with a [[Engine/Legends|drive system]]. ==History== ===Captured by Darth Vader=== {{Quote|Where are the stolen plans? I know they're on this ship!|Darth Vader|}} During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the ''Tantive V'' participated in the Rebel Alliance's [[Operation Skyhook|campaign]] to secure stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|technical readouts]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. After the Rebels succeeded in [[Battle of Toprawa|capturing and transmitting]] the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] data to [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] aboard the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Consular ship/Legends|consular ship]] ''Tantive IV'', Organa began racing home to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to finalize the safe acquisition of the stolen data. However, the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], commanded by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], pursued the Rebel corvette in an effort to capture Organa and recover the stolen plans.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' [[File:AbsoBarBinks.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Abso Bar Binks emerges from the ''Tantive V'' to stall Darth Vader.]] As an exact lookalike of Organa's ''Tantive IV'', the ''Tantive V'' provided the perfect diversion to stall the ''Devastator'' as it gave chase. Crewed only by a group of unarmed [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]], the ''Tantive V'' lured the ''Devastator'' in pursuit of itself and away from the ''Tantive IV'', thus buying [[Time/Legends|time]] for Vader's true target to temporarily slip away with the plans. Ignorant of the ruse, the ''Devastator'' captured the ''Tantive V'' via [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] and drew the neutralized vessel into its main [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], where a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] force, led by Vader himself, prepared to board the corvette. Vader's stormtroopers covered each of the ''Tantive V''{{'s}} hatches and jury-rigged open the corvette's main hatch when the Rebel counterattack came. A [[Cloak/Legends|hooded]] figure—in reality, the Gungan [[Abso Bar Binks]], son of former Imperial [[Senator/Legends|Senator]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], personally tasked with stalling Vader—tossed two small [[Booma/Legends|boomas]] into the ''Devastator'' from inside the ''Tantive V''{{'s}} breached hatch, creating an explosion and an effective smoke screen among the stormtroopers. ===The perfect decoy=== {{Quote|If yousa still wants da ambassador, she'sa not on dis ship. And neithers are da secret plans.|Abso Bar Binks, to Darth Vader|}} In the ensuing confusion, Binks slipped through the hatchway into the ''Devastator'' and began to attack the distracted stormtroopers, who refrained from firing on the hooded figure in fear that it might be Organa herself, whom they hoped to capture rather than [[Death/Legends|kill]]. Upon eventually learning the identity—and ineptitude—of the hooded attacker, Vader quickly ordered his stormtroopers to board the ''Tantive V'' and bring him the Death Star plans. However, the stormtroopers soon reported to Vader that the data they sought was nowhere to be found aboard the ''Tantive V'', and all they had uncovered was the corvette's unarmed Gungan crew. [[File:Tantive_V_released-SWMB.jpg|thumb|right|290px|The ''Devastator'' releases the ''Tantive V'' back into space.]] At that moment, Binks revealed the deception to Vader: The ''Tantive V'' had been a diversion all along, and he{{InsiderCite|131|We Need to Talk About Jar Jar}} had single-handedly manufactured the opportunity that Organa and the ''Tantive IV'' needed to escape. The stormtroopers booted Binks back aboard the ''Tantive V'', but Vader refrained from issuing the order to eliminate the Gungans, considering them too worthless on which to waste [[Blaster/Legends|blasterfire]]. The ''Devastator'' released the ''Tantive V'' back into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and continued pursuit of Organa. Despite the ''Tantive V''{{'s}} efforts, the ''Devastator'' caught up with and [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|captured]] the ''Tantive IV'' an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] later over the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. However, Vader failed to recover the Death Star plans, eventually leading to the battlestation's destruction at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].{{Film|IV}} ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|Lord Vader! We can't find them anywhere!''"
"''What?''"
"''All we've found are unarmed Gungans.|Darth Vader's stormtroopers failed to find the Death Star plans aboard the ''Tantive V''|}} [[File:Tantive_V_Gungans-SWMB.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Members of the ''Tantive V''{{'s}} unarmed Gungan crew are captured.]] During the mission to delay [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's pursuit of Princess Leia Organa aboard the ''Tantive IV'', the ''Tantive V'' was manned by a crew of unarmed Gungans, including Abso Bar Binks, the Rebel son of Jar Jar Binks. Binks and his fellow Gungans fooled the ''Devastator''{{'s}} crew into believing that the ''Tantive V'' carried the princess and the stolen Death Star plans. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Tantive V'' appeared in the illustrated story [[Tantive IV (comic story)|''Tantive IV'']], created by the artist known as [[Formalin]] and published in [[Tokyopop]]'s [[October]] [[2006]] ''[[Star Wars Manga: Black]]'' trade paperback [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]]. All stories from ''Star Wars Manga: Black'' were considered [[Canon|non-canon]] within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=Tantive IV (comic story)|book=Star Wars Manga: Black|stext=''Tantive IV''}} {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Tantive V }} [[Category:CR90 corvettes]] [[Category:Starships of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]]
